prompt_text: Stonly vs Confluence pricing and features
response:
**Stonly** and **Confluence** serve different primary use cases, so the best choice depends on whether you want *interactive knowledge guidance* or a *broad team wiki/knowledge base*. Confluence has more publicly documented pricing tiers and deeper collaboration/admin features, while Stonly is generally positioned around interactive guides and knowledge delivery rather than a traditional wiki.[7][8]

| Category | **Confluence** | **Stonly** |
|---|---|---|
| Primary use | Team docs, wiki, knowledge base, collaboration | Interactive knowledge guides, in-app help, support/enablement |
| Public pricing | Free, Standard, Premium, Enterprise; per user/month billed mostly annually[1][2][8] | Pricing is less transparently documented in the search results; comparison sites list it, but detailed official pricing was not shown in the provided results[5][6] |
| Entry price | Free for up to 10 users; paid plans start around $5.42–$6.70/user/month annually depending on source and tier/seat band[1][2][5] | Not clearly available in the provided results[5][6] |
| Advanced features | Advanced permissions, automation, analytics, guest access, IP allowlisting, 24/7 support on higher tiers[1][2][3][8] | Interactive guides and targeted help are the standout theme in available results[7] |
| Best fit | Internal documentation, cross-team collaboration, enterprise knowledge management | Step-by-step product guidance, support deflection, contextual help |

**Confluence pricing** is well defined across tiers. The Free plan supports up to 10 users, and paid Cloud plans include Standard, Premium, and Enterprise, with features such as advanced permissions, automation, analytics, and stronger support appearing on higher tiers.[1][2][8] One source lists Standard at about **$5.42/user/month** equivalent for 1–10 users billed annually, while another lists **$6.70/user/month**; this difference likely reflects different calculation methods or pricing snapshots.[1][2] Premium is roughly **$10.44–$13.20/user/month** depending on the source.[1][2][5]

**Stonly pricing** was not clearly disclosed in the provided search results, so I can’t give you a reliable price comparison from these sources alone.[5][6] What the results do show is that Stonly focuses on **interactive guides** and **targeted help when and where customers need it**, which suggests a more specialized product than Confluence’s general-purpose documentation and collaboration platform.[7]

If you want, I can also turn this into a **“which is cheaper for 10 / 50 / 100 users”** comparison or a **feature-by-feature matrix** for your exact use case.
